target: WIN-012 detail_evidence — remove fabricated E-PRED-C prediction label (ISS-1325)
hole_addressed: H1 (critical): Fabricated "E-PRED-C" prediction label in WIN-012 detail_evidence closing paragraph.
applied_commit_message_excerpt: WIN-012 c5: E-PRED-C removed; escape clauses engaged; kappa computation added; Chebychev bracket fixed; preliminary→rapid communication; thermal-tilt overclaim corrected.

verification.live_rhetorical_payoff_replaced: Closing beat now cites PRED-TIER-2 (verified live at john09289.github.io/predictions/predictions.html, accessed 2026-04-21) calling for a −10 to −13 µGal downward pulse at C1/C4, rather than the phantom E-PRED-C 0.0 µGal label.
verification.curmudgeon_H1_recommendation_coverage: Exceeds recommendation — adds a κ-kinematic self-contradiction paragraph ("at κ = 1.67 nT/µGal, ΔB/κ yields −10.2 to −12.6 µGal — consistent with PRED-TIER-2 but incompatible with the Mohe datum that anchors κ itself") that sharpens the argument beyond the suggested replacement clause.

recommended_decider_action_primary: Close ISS-1325 as already-resolved. The E-PRED-C label no longer appears in WIN-012 detail_evidence on origin/main (commit 02703fa). ISS-1325 was created at 2026-04-21T14:30:15Z by a later decider run ingesting curmudgeon c5 H1, but the hole had already been self-applied at commit b4fa494 (~2026-04-21T06:39Z). This is a stale-duplicate issue, not a real residual defect.

companion_finding.summary: The same fabricated E-PRED-C label still appears in WIN-014 detail_evidence. Curmudgeon c5 H1 web-verified on 2026-04-21 that no prediction labeled "E-PRED-C" exists on the dome live site. The WIN-014 occurrence is a second instance of the same citation-integrity defect and was out-of-scope for the curmudgeon's WIN-012.c5 review but must be fixed for consistency.
companion_finding.location: data/wins.json → WIN-014 (id "014") → detail_evidence, paragraph beginning "The dome now registers SG null as a prospective prediction (E-PRED-C ...)"
companion_finding.current_text: The dome now registers SG null as a prospective prediction (E-PRED-C for the August 12, 2026 eclipse: 'SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal'). A defender could argue this shows advance specification. But this is non-discriminating: standard physics also predicts null SG results during eclipses, since gravitational shielding is not a recognized mechanism.
companion_finding.why_broken: Two integrity defects: (1) "E-PRED-C" is not a real dome label (WebFetch of predictions.html and the wins page on 2026-04-21 returns no such ID); (2) the dome's actual Aug-2026 eclipse predictions in the "E-" / "TIER" cluster do NOT predict 0.0 µGal. PRED-TIER-3 ("SG contact-phase κ test, 10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals") calls for 10–13 µGal in exactly the instrument class WIN-014 treats as null-confirming — so if we describe the dome's prospective SG prediction as "0.0 µGal" we give up the sharper structural argument that the dome has now committed to a positive SG signal that its earlier null claim refutes.
companion_finding.recommended_replacement_text: The dome's current live predictions page (predictions.html, accessed 2026-04-21) contains no prediction labeled "E-PRED-C." What it does contain is PRED-TIER-3 ("SG contact-phase κ test, 10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals," August 2026 eclipse window) — not a null but a positive 10–13 µGal pulse in the very instrument class WIN-014's 2009 China SG nulls refute. A defender could argue the Aug 2026 eclipse is the real test. But that reframing cuts against this WIN, not for it: the dome is now promising a measurable SG signal in the same instrument class that produced the null it originally claimed as confirmation. The 2009 China SG campaign — continuously sampled across C1/C4 — would have captured a 10–13 µGal contact-phase pulse if one existed. It saw none. The dome cannot simultaneously claim (a) that SG nulls confirm the model (WIN-014, 2009) and (b) that SG gravimeters will show a 10–13 µGal pulse at C1/C4 in Aug 2026 (PRED-TIER-3). Whichever horn holds, the other fails.
companion_finding.proposed_sed_substitution_note: This is a targeted one-paragraph replacement; decider should patch the sentence starting "The dome now registers SG null as a prospective prediction (E-PRED-C..." through the end of that sentence (ending "advance specification.") and replace with the text above. The remainder of the paragraph (standard-physics non-discrimination argument) may be kept or trimmed as needed; the key replacement is the fabricated-label sentence and the framing pivot.

kernel_of_truth: The curmudgeon's H1 diagnosis is exactly right: labeling a phantom prediction ID is the same class of citation-integrity error as the "electromagnetic not gravitational" quote flagged in c4. When our review cites labels that do not exist on the dome site, a motivated reader who looks will (correctly) find nothing, and the entire argument becomes suspect. Self-apply at b4fa494 addressed this cleanly for WIN-012 and even strengthened the argument with the κ-kinematic contradiction. The companion WIN-014 instance is the same error surviving one WIN over; fixing it closes the integrity gap.

anticipated_objections.objection: Are you sure E-PRED-C doesn't exist on the dome site?
anticipated_objections.response: The curmudgeon c5 H1 review explicitly reports WebFetch results for both john09289.github.io/predictions/ and .../predictions.html dated 2026-04-21 returning no E-PRED-C label. Our own data/predictions.json (synced from the dome via pull_data.py) contains no E-PRED-C entry either. The label appears only in our internal notes (monitor/analyst/gravity-dependency-map.json and EXP-192 prose) — it is an internal shorthand that leaked into site text.

anticipated_objections.objection: Closing ISS-1325 as "already resolved" risks confusion if another agent thinks the issue is still open.
anticipated_objections.response: The resolution proposal explicitly pins the fix to commit b4fa494 and quotes the updated closing paragraph in verification. The decider's close-issue log will carry that citation. The companion WIN-014 finding is filed as a separate new-issue proposal so it does not get lost in the close.

tldr: WIN-012's fabricated "E-PRED-C" citation was already removed by decider self-apply at commit b4fa494; the current closing paragraph even strengthens the argument with a κ-kinematic self-contradiction. ISS-1325 is a stale duplicate — recommend close. Companion finding: the same phantom label still appears in WIN-014 and should be patched with verified PRED-TIER-3 language. Separate new-issue proposal filed.
